basis.canary_executable: provider is allowed only for explicit canary or controlled routes unless promoted
basis.catalog_routable: provider has at least one catalog-supported job type
basis.production_route: provider may be selected by normal planning for its supported job types
basis.registered: adapter or policy entry is present in the generated provider catalog

notes: RunPod execute uses an existing serverless endpoint when one is configured or discoverable.
RunPod smoke execute uses a bounded Pod HTTP worker canary and writes the standard artifact contract.
gpu-job-control does not create paid pods automatically.
Serverless endpoint creation requires a concrete template_id and gpu_ids.
Use workers_min=0 and workers_standby=0 unless paid warm capacity is explicitly intended.
Worker must write result.json, metrics.json, verify.json, stdout.log, stderr.log.

public_endpoint_candidates.name: RunPod official worker-vllm
notes: Use OpenAI-compatible endpoint path when deployed.
Prefer this over bespoke workers for vLLM-compatible models.

best_for: fast reuse of public worker template
public_endpoint_candidates.name: SvenBrnn/runpod-worker-ollama
notes: Attach an approved network volume so models are cached across cold starts.
Use workersMin=0 and workersStandby=0 unless paid warm capacity is explicitly accepted.

public_endpoint_candidates.name: RunPod Ollama tutorial image
notes: Official RunPod documentation recommends network volume caching to reduce repeated model downloads.

canary_sequence: clean pre-guard; fail if active pods or warm serverless capacity exist
create serverless template from verified ASR diarization image
create endpoint with workersMin=0, workersStandby=0, workersMax=1
submit ASR diarization workspace canary payload
verify result.json, metrics.json, verify.json, stdout.log, stderr.log
clean post-guard; fail on warm capacity or resource residue
parse contract probe and require all workspace observation categories

gpu_selection.rule: gpuIds accepts RunPod GPU pool IDs; concrete GPU type names are only valid as exclusions prefixed with '-'

cost_guard_policy: workers_min must remain 0 unless user explicitly chooses paid warm capacity.
workers_standby must remain 0 unless user explicitly chooses paid warm capacity.
gpu-job guard blocks execution if warm capacity is detected.

command_template: serverless endpoint receives input.prompt, input.system_prompt, input.model, input.max_tokens
response must include text/response/output/generated_text/answer or OpenAI-style choices

metadata.route_explanation: selected provider 'runpod' for gpu_profile 'llm_heavy' from candidates [runpod]. reason: provider explicitly requested

generate_result.text: RunPod Pod GPU canary response. mode=llm; prompt_sha256_prefix=dc6d872ab93b884e; This confirms HTTP generation path, GPU probe, artifact contract, and teardown.

volume_probe.error: volume path is not a directory
volume path is not a directory

cleanup_sequence: create pod only after clean pre-guard
observe desiredStatus/runtime until ready or timeout
run post-guard and require no active pods

worker_canary.asr_diarization_condition: imports faster_whisper, pyannote.audio, matplotlib and observes HF_TOKEN without running full ASR
worker_canary.success_condition: HTTP 200 JSON with ok=true and nvidia-smi exit_code=0

basis.production_route: provider may be selected by normal planning for its supported job types
basis.registered: adapter or policy entry is present in the generated provider catalog
